rulings.Case Title: BHSD 505 PROPCO LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. HOSIE RICE, LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 4, 2025 line 1. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. The issues in the Notice of Motion are not stated "verbatim" in the separate statement, in violation of California Rule of Court 3.1350(b). =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BHSD 505 PROPCO LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. HOSIE RICE, LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Excess Pages Of Plaintiffs' Reply Brief In Support Of Its Motion For Summary Adjudication For Exceeding The Page Limit
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 4, 2025 line 1. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE EXCESS PAGES OF PLAINTIFFS' REPLY BRIEF IN SUPPORT OF ITS MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FOR EXCEEDING THE PAGE LIMIT is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005. The Court notes that the Memorandum of Points and Authorities in support of the Reply, excluding the captions page, Table of Contents and Table of Authorities, is 10 pages long. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DON RAMON'S REAL ESTATE, LLC VS. DE LA MANCHA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Mandatory Relief From Default
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 4, 2025 line 2. DEFENDANT DE LA MANCHA'S MOTION FOR MANDATORY RELIEF FROM DEFAULT is GRANTED based on mandatory relief provisions. Answer filed on October 17, 2022 is reinstated. No later than October 4, 2025 Defendant shall provide responses to all outstanding discovery served on Defendant's prior counsel before the suspension on January 27, 2023. Plaintiff may file any proper discovery motions upon the receipt of the discovery responses. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: COLOMBIA MISSION BAY LLC VS. YEON KYUNG LEE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enter Judgmetn Based On Default Under Settlement Agreement And, In The Event Of Default, Stipulation For Entry Of Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 4, 2025 line 4. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO ENTER JUDGMENT BASED ON DEFAULT UNDER SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT is DENIED. Moving party failed to establish entitlement to a judgment based on breach of paragraph 4(a) of the stipulation. See paragraph 10 of the Situation ("In the event Defendant commits a breach of paragraph 3 or 7 of this agreement...") Plaintiff does not point to any other paragraphs of the stipulation as the basis for entry of judgment. The Court also notes that there is no competent admissible evidence regarding failure to make payment. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. MOHAMED BENZERGA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Enforcement Of Settlement Agreement And Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Ccp 664.6
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 4, 2025 line 5. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ENFORCEMENT OF SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AND ENTRY OF JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO CCP 664.6 HEARING REQUIRED for status updates regarding defendant's rental assistance and disability applications, as well as defendant's request for reasonable accommodation. No later than 9:00 a.m. on September 4, 2025, plaintiff to file a supplemental declaration regarding the current status of defendant's request for reasonable accommodation. To the extent plaintiff implies that it is excused from responding to the request due to the timing, the Court notes that plaintiff provides no authority for this proposition. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
